title: New England Aquarium to celebrate World Ocean Day with free waterfront events on Sunday
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: New England Aquarium hosts World Ocean Day with free events at Central Wharf from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.
Featured attractions include a life-sized humpback whale installation and an 'Ask a Scientist' booth with marine experts.
Free shuttle transportation available from Dorchester and Roxbury through Boys & Girls Clubs, pre-registration required.

title: Review & setlist: 5 Seconds of Summer keep the fans at the center of an unforgettable show
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 5 Seconds of Summer performed at TD Garden on June 5, 2026, as part of their 'Everyone's A Star!' World Tour.
The concert featured six acts with theatrical storytelling, humor, and fan interactions like a fan-voted surprise song.
5SOS's setlist included new and old hits like 'No. 1 Obsession' and 'Youngblood,' with a focus on fan engagement.

title: What 17th-century advice seekers had in common with us
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein to discuss historical advice columns on June 30 at Boston Public Library.
Historian Mary Beth Norton to join Goldstein, discussing her book on 17th-century advice.
The event, exploring advice from the 1600s to today, is free to the public.

title: New on Netflix and more: Top streaming picks for June 2026
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix's June offerings include 'The Big Lebowski,' 'Creed,' 'Inside Man,' and 'Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse.'
Disney+ features Pixar's new movie 'Hoppers,' while A24's 'Pillion' and Apple's series 'Cape Fear' are also streaming.
Prime Video debuts 'Spider-Noir' starring Nicolas Cage, with Peacock streaming 'Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World.'

title: Ex-Yankee Sonny Gray earns first victory against New York as member of Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray earned his first win against the Yankees as a Red Sox, pitching 6.1 innings in a 5-3 victory.
Gray, who played for New York in 2017-2018, expressed dislike for the Yankees and chose Boston in the offseason.
Gray's record this season is 7-1 with a 3.20 ERA, contributing significantly to the Red Sox rotation.

title: Willson Contreras rides highs and lows of Red Sox win over Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a 2-run homer in the Red Sox's win against the Yankees, boosting his season total to 13 home runs.
Contreras showed visible frustration by spiking a Gatorade cooler after a collision with teammate Andruw Monasterio in the eighth inning.
Interim manager Chad Tracy praised Contreras's leadership and work ethic, highlighting his impact on younger Red Sox players.

title: Severe thunderstorms could hit Mass. Saturday evening, forecasters say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Severe thunderstorms with damaging winds and hail are forecast for Massachusetts Saturday after 2 p.m. per NWS.
Greatest risk for severe weather is in areas west and northwest of Interstate 95, NWS Boston reports.
Thunderstorms may lead to localized flooding and are expected to diminish by 10 p.m. Saturday.

title: Nantasket Beach stayed quiet despite fears of a social media-fueled ‘takeover’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hull police increased presence at Nantasket Beach after social media rumors of a takeover event.
No incidents were reported at Nantasket Beach, and police described the atmosphere as quieter than usual.
State troopers noted Carson Beach as a potential new location, but no gatherings or arrests were recorded.

title: Red Sox’ Payton Tolle talks ‘playing with joy’ for self, team: ‘I try to come with a smile every day’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Payton Tolle made his MLB debut for the Boston Red Sox on August 29, 2025, against the Pittsburgh Pirates.
Tolle's mother, who passed away in 2024, encouraged him to play with joy, influencing his passionate style.
Tolle uses humor and emotion in postgame interviews, adding unique energy to the last-place 2026 Red Sox.

title: Colin Cowherd: Celtics future feels ‘weird’ compared to sustainable Knicks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Colin Cowherd criticized the Celtics' future, citing concerns over high cap consumption by Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum.
Cowherd highlighted the Knicks' salary cap flexibility, credited to Jalen Brunson's team-friendly contract, as a long-term advantage.
Cowherd questioned Brown's optimism about the season, noting conflicts and early playoff exit despite their higher seed.
